# Key re-use between users: sesam refuses to create that state in the first
# place (the add is rejected), and `sesam verify --key-reuse` is the backstop
# for a state that bypassed the normal path (e.g. a hand-edited/forged log).

exec git init
exec git config user.email test@sesam.dev
exec git config user.name 'Sesam Test'

exec sesam init --user admin
exec git add -A
exec git commit -m 'init sesam'

# A clean repo shares no keys.
exec sesam verify --key-reuse
stderr 'Key re-use: ok'

# Add bob with his key.
exec sesam tell --user bob --recipient $BOB_PUBKEY --group dev

# Giving carol a key already registered to bob is rejected at add time.
! exec sesam tell --user carol --recipient $BOB_PUBKEY --group dev
stderr 'already has recipient'

# So the repo stays clean under the key-reuse check.
exec sesam verify --key-reuse
stderr 'Key re-use: ok'
